objective: A fresh agent starting from the master skill produces the full routing chain with at most one reference drill.

cases.prompt: You are orchestrating from Codex. One board node contains four independent, already-specified migration leaves with one shared output schema and a deterministic join. Explain the complete route before dispatch. Preserve executor=workflow if appropriate, but use only runtime primitives actually supported by this host. State what evidence must exist before in_flight and what happens after every runtime becomes terminal.
must_observe: Chooses workflow as a planning responsibility because the leaves need deterministic fan-out and join.
Does not claim that Codex provides the Claude Workflow runtime or call Workflow, agent(), parallel(), or pipeline().
Maps execution to Codex-native subagents, terminal sessions, cloud tasks, or independent board tasks and requires real handles.
Separates target surface, effect floor, exact qualification, same-floor fallback, handle, and endpoint verification.

cases.prompt: Route an independent architecture and irreversible data-boundary decision. The registry labels one exact target as candidate for O, but its role certification and live payer-bound admission are unknown. A certified T1 target is ample. Give the complete routing record and decide whether to dispatch now.
must_observe: Sets effect_floor=O before considering named targets.
Treats candidate as unqualified because exact O certification and live admission are unknown.
Does not downgrade the original task to the ample T1 target.
Stops, scopes a reversible evidence-gathering step, replans, or surfaces the block instead of dispatching.

cases.prompt: A complete spec and acceptance contract define one implementation leaf. Two exact targets are certified T1 and admitted: target A is lower latency but tight, target B has ample headroom and slightly higher integration cost. Choose and describe a same-floor fallback. The selected worker returns a real session handle and later exits zero while reporting tests green. Explain every state transition through final task closure.
must_observe: Chooses T1 from task shape and keeps every fallback at T1.
Ranks only the two already-qualified targets and records the capacity/integration tradeoff.
Requires bind and link of the real handle before in_flight.
Treats process terminal and self-reported green tests as inputs, then independently verifies artifact, diff, tests, and acceptance before done.
